expectations.text: States the lex specialis principle: under NIS2 Art. 4, sector-specific Union legal acts with at-least-equivalent effect take precedence, and DORA (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554) is that act for financial entities including credit institutions
expectations.evidence: 'NIS2 contains an explicit sector-specific lex specialis clause (Art. 4 of the NIS2 Directive...): where a sector-specific EU legal act requires entities to adopt cybersecurity risk-management measures or to report incidents, and those requirements are at least equivalent in effect... the sector-specific act applies instead... DORA is named as exactly such an act for the financial sector.' DORA is explicitly cited as 'Regulation (EU) 2022/2554' in section 1.

expectations.text: Specifies WHICH provisions are displaced: DORA applies instead of NIS2 Art. 21 (cybersecurity risk-management measures) and Art. 23 (incident reporting) for banks
expectations.evidence: 'Art. 21 risk management measures — DORA's ICT risk management framework... is deemed equivalent and displaces NIS2 Art. 21 for you... Art. 23 incident reporting — DORA's major ICT-related incident reporting regime... displaces NIS2 Art. 23.'

expectations.text: Notes what remains relevant from NIS2 despite DORA precedence (e.g., banks remain listed entities / national registration and information-sharing frameworks may still apply; supervision of ICT risk sits with financial authorities under DORA)
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'National critical-infrastructure registration, CSIRT liaison, Member State-specific NIS2 transposition duties | NIS2 | Still applies — this is not an ICT risk-management or incident-reporting obligation per se, so it isn't displaced' and 'Sector interdependency / cross-sector supply chain coordination (Art. 22 coordinated risk assessments...) | NIS2 | Still applies.'

expectations.text: Recommends a single integrated programme built on DORA's requirements (ICT risk management framework, incident classification and reporting, digital operational resilience testing including TLPT, ICT third-party risk, information sharing) mapped against NIS2 for residual obligations — not two separate programmes
expectations.evidence: 'You need one integrated compliance programme, anchored in DORA, with a NIS2 gap-check layered on top — not two parallel programmes.' The table lists ICT risk management, incident reporting, 'Digital operational resilience testing (incl. TLPT)', and ICT third-party/concentration risk all anchored to DORA, each cross-referenced against NIS2 relevance.

expectations.text: Any incident-reporting specifics given match DORA's regime (major ICT-related incidents reported to the financial competent authority with initial/intermediate/final reports) rather than NIS2 CSIRT reporting
expectations.evidence: 'Art. 23 incident reporting — DORA's major ICT-related incident reporting regime (its own timelines and templates to your competent authority, not the NIS2 24h/72h/1-month CSIRT track) displaces NIS2 Art. 23. You report ICT incidents the DORA way.' No NIS2-specific 24h/72h/1-month timeline is presented as applicable to the bank's own reporting obligations.
